name: GT-bh-cstar
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:458-466 (1.3.2), VERBATIM: "$\mathscr{A}$  is called a Banach algebra if  $\|xy\| \le \|x\| \|y\|$  for all  $x, y \in \mathscr{A}$ ." and "If H is a complex Hilbert space then B(H), the bounded linear operators on H, is a  $C^*$  algebra in the usual operator norm". Hence in B(H): ||xy||<=||x|| ||y|| (associative product), and the operator norm is the order-unit norm on B(H)_sa.
